title: Fall 2022 OSU operating list — contacts, OSIG pitch, CS162 — Sept 2022
html:
TL;DR

  • Operating list as Yahir lands back in Corvallis post-Vietnam: open US Bank card, figure out Linux terminal stuff (early CS-toolchain pain point), start chipping at the OSIG pitch, switch insurance with valeria, call david, get contacts via mom, marinate chicken, send JCEF the mandarin class fee (first hint of the China-prep direction).
  • A discrete sub-block: CS162 TA questions — "Once code is written, how should I look at it, and how should one submit it?", "How intricate should the code outline be?", "Am I behind on the lab if it's not done before we enter class?", "Any textbooks?"
  • The Linux + CS162 questions are the earliest captured CS-coursework operating tempo — Yahir mid-novice, asking baseline mechanics questions.

Key quote

"Once code is written, how should I look at it, and how should one submit it?"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Mandarin class fee → JCEF → first paper trail of the china chapter funding scaffolding.
  • CS162 TA-question naïveté is a delta marker — by 2024 12 10 osu graduation Yahir is 9 electives from a CS degree and by 2024 07 04 questions mega notebook he's bossing his own cursor-app grind. Worth bookmarking the early-baseline.
  • OSIG pitch ties to 2022 04 28 osig workbook and 2022 06 14 get josh allen's OSIG admin tasks — together they form Yahir's OSIG-internship operating block.

Open questions

  • What was the OSIG pitch about?
  • Did the Linux terminal stuff get figured out via course or self-teach?

Wikilinks
